name: Kuma Service Mesh API
what_it_does: CNCF-backed open-source universal service mesh with REST API for managing traffic policies, mTLS, observability, and multi-zone deployments across Kubernetes and VM workloads.
use_cases: Applying traffic routing policies (retry, timeout, circuit breaker) across microservices via API
Managing mTLS certificate rotation and identity policies programmatically
Querying service mesh traffic metrics and health for SRE automation agents
Configuring zero-trust network policies between services without code changes
Managing multi-cluster and multi-zone service mesh deployments from a central control plane
not_for: Organizations without Kubernetes or service mesh expertise
Simple monolithic applications without service-to-service communication needs
Teams running Istio (Kuma and Istio have different policy models)
best_when: An agent manages service mesh policies for multi-cluster Kubernetes deployments needing zero-trust networking and traffic management.
avoid_when: You already run Istio and switching service meshes would be disruptive.

auth.notes: Kuma Global Control Plane uses token-based authentication. Multi-zone tokens for connecting remote zones. RBAC support in Kuma Enterprise (Kong Mesh). Open-source version has basic auth.

pricing.notes: Kuma is CNCF Incubating project created by Kong. Kong Mesh is the enterprise distribution with RBAC, OPA policies, FIPS compliance, and support.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted; determined by control plane resources

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documentation is available and covers policy CRUD operations. Error responses are JSON. kumactl CLI is the primary interface but REST API is complete.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Policy creation and updates are idempotent — applying the same policy resource produces deterministic state.
known_agent_gotchas: Kuma policies use a different targeting model than Istio — zone, mesh, and service hierarchy must be understood
Multi-zone deployments add complexity — global vs zone control plane operations are different
mTLS policies affect all service communication — careful rollout strategy required to avoid outages
Data plane proxy (Envoy) restart may be required for some policy changes

security_score_components.security_notes: Built-in mTLS between all services. CNCF governance. Apache 2.0 open-source. Enterprise version adds OPA policy, FIPS 140-2. Token-based control plane auth is basic in open-source version.
